mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad demand contraction that depresses corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit over additive proxies in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply chain bottlenecks that trigger short-term market panic, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of industrial production decline episodes, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate surpasses 5.0 percent, borrowing costs severely constrain corporate leverage and equity risk premiums, forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in a 3-year rolling IC test.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and falling total nonfarm payrolls confirms a structural labor market breakdown that erodes long-term earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields positive 60-day returns in >58% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding gross domestic product increases corporate revenue expectations and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of GDP expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 2017 baseline level indicates severe capacity utilization constraints that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where INDPRO drops below 100, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during demand-driven regimes.
